ideas.title: Explicit installer state machine
ideas.description: Model installation as Preflight -> Select Mode -> Select Targets -> Select Components -> Confirm Backup -> Execute -> Summarize.
ideas.rationale: This is the structural difference between Fabric today and ccw install.

ideas.title: Install plan object
ideas.description: Build a typed install plan before any writes occur, then execute from that plan.
ideas.rationale: Makes dry-run, confirmation, and later dashboard reuse much easier.

ideas.title: Unified stage reporting
ideas.description: Normalize each install stage to the same result shape so both wizard and non-wizard modes can share summary rendering.
ideas.rationale: Reduces duplicated output logic between current init and future install UX.

key_findings: The core missing abstraction in Fabric is an install plan/state machine.
Today the execution and presentation layers are intertwined in `init.ts`.
A breaking redesign can collapse scan, plan, confirm, and execute into a single canonical init flow.
challenged_assumptions: A better install experience is not just an i18n/output problem; it is also a flow-modeling problem.
open_questions: Should the plan abstraction be introduced first, before any prompt library is added?
